checks.invariant: Private manifests are supersets and public manifests are safe projections.
checks.observed: PrivateManifest can carry private and public entities, while to_public_manifest_dict emits only public-safe nodes and relationships.
checks.expected: Private nodes and local-only fields stay out of public output.

checks.invariant: Unknown visibility and unknown projection behavior fail closed.
checks.observed: Visibility and projection behavior are parsed through constrained enums; unknown values are rejected at load time.

checks.invariant: Public projection should strip private URLs, internal paths, local annotations, and private-only relationships.
checks.observed: Projection tests and PMV detectors cover private URLs, internal paths, env files, endpoint overrides, runtime hints, and non-public relationship leakage.
checks.expected: No private leakage in generated public manifests.

checks.invariant: Public publication should not have an accidental unvalidated path.
checks.observed: project-public always validates; unsafe generation is isolated on project-public-unsafe and prints a warning.
checks.expected: No unvalidated publication path on the normal command surface.

checks.invariant: Projection rules should be explicit and declarative rather than living only in procedural code.
checks.observed: Projection behavior now lives in explicit model and schema fields on entities and relationships.
checks.expected: Projection rules visible in model or schema data with fail-closed semantics.

checks.invariant: Deterministic placeholder redaction applies only if placeholder-redaction output is implemented.
checks.observed: The current implementation emits public-safe aliases and redacted stubs but still follows a drop-first design for most private data.
checks.expected: Placeholder tests remain scoped to fields that actually use alias or stub behavior.
